compiled_state.title: Deep-tier opus arch reviewer scores failed with only minor findings
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Bug Description

The dso:code-reviewer-deep-arch (opus) agent returned REVIEW_RESULT: failed with MIN_SCORE=2 despite all 3 findings being severity=minor. Per the review scoring rubric, minor-only findings should produce a passing review (score 4+), not a failure (score 2).

## Root Cause

The opus arch agent's prompt does not explicitly enforce the constraint that score must be consistent with finding severity. A reviewer can report all-minor findings but still assign low dimension scores, creating an inconsistency between the qualitative assessment (minor issues only) and the quantitative score (failed).

## Expected Behavior

When all findings are severity=minor (no critical or important), the review should:
- Score all dimensions >= 4 (minor findings reduce from 5 to 4, not lower)
- Return REVIEW_RESULT: passed
- The orchestrator can then proceed with commit, noting minor suggestions

## Observed Behavior

- All 3 findings: severity=minor
- MIN_SCORE=2
- REVIEW_RESULT: failed
- This forced the orchestrator into escalation/user-approval despite no substantive code issues

## Fix

Add explicit scoring consistency guidance to the dso:code-reviewer-deep-arch agent definition:
- 'If all findings are severity=minor, no dimension should score below 4'
- 'If any dimension scores below pass_threshold, at least one finding targeting that dimension must be severity=important or critical'
- This mirrors the constraint already implicit in the scoring scale (4 = 'Strong — meets requirements, minor polish suggestions')

## Affected Files
- plugins/dso/agents/code-reviewer-deep-arch.md (add scoring consistency rule)
- Potentially all reviewer agents (code-reviewer-light.md, code-reviewer-standard.md, deep specialists) for consistency
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). Root cause: opus arch reviewer agent definition lacks score-severity consistency constraint.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
